gate_type: non-default option required — socketPath in request options not typical user-controlled input
reasoning: The exported API (module.exports and module.exports.requestFilterHandler) is public, so the patched agent is reachable. However, triggering the Unix socket bypass requires the caller to make an HTTP request with socketPath set in the request options (e.g., http.request({socketPath: '/var/run/docker.sock', ...})). This is a non-default, non-typical usage pattern. The library's normal use case is SSRF protection against attacker-controlled URLs — the attacker controls a URL string, not the socketPath option. For socketPath to be attacker-controlled, the application would need to expose a code path where user input populates the socketPath field of an http.request call, which is an exceptionally rare application pattern. Additionally, Unix socket access already implies local system access; an attacker who can supply a socketPath value has effectively already bypassed the security boundary the library is meant to protect. The bypass is real but requires a non-default configuration and an unusual threat model, so gate_breachable is false and severity is downgraded to informational.
